insights.headline: US and Iran warn they are ready for war as Pakistan ceasefire talks in limbo
insights.summary: The United States and Iran on Monday each warned they were ready for war as the clock ticked on a ceasefire, with uncertainty on talks that US President Donald Trump had announced would resume in Pakistan. The White House said Vice-President J.D. Vance was ready to fly back to the Pakistani capital.

insights.headline: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after issuing tsunami warning
insights.summary: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week. This follows an initial quake that triggered tsunami warnings, placing the country on maximum alert.

insights.headline: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, handing reins to firm's hardware leader
insights.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook is stepping down from the job that he inherited from the late Steve Jobs, ending a nearly 15-year reign that saw the company's market value soar by more than US$3.6 trillion. Cook, 65, will turn CEO duties over to Apple's head of hardware engineering, John Ternus, on September 1.

insights.headline: The World Is Paying the Price for America's War
insights.summary: Conflict with Iran hurts American wallets, but it's far more devastating for people in the global south. The analysis examines how the US-Israel war on Iran is driving up energy prices and destabilising economies across Asia and Africa.

insights.headline: The Strategic Aftershocks of Trump's Iran War
insights.summary: The consequences will be felt long after the fighting ends. Foreign Policy examines the long-term geopolitical fallout from the US-Israel war on Iran, including implications for Israel, oil markets, and US global standing.

insights.headline: How 50 days of the Iran war led to the loss of $50 billion worth of oil
insights.summary: Reuters analysis details the massive economic impact of the Iran war on global oil markets, with 50 days of conflict resulting in $50 billion in lost oil production and exports.

insights.headline: Iran replenishes launchers at higher rate than pre-war, guards commander says
insights.summary: A Revolutionary Guard commander claims Iran is replenishing missile and launcher systems at a higher rate than before the war, suggesting significant industrial resilience or external support.

insights.headline: Pro-Iran hackers appear to increase critical infrastructure cyberattacks
insights.summary: One group claimed responsibility for hacking the Los Angeles Metro as the federal government warns of vulnerabilities. Pro-Iran hacktivist groups are escalating cyberattacks on Western critical infrastructure.

insights.headline: Trump boasts on 'winning war' on social media posts
insights.summary: Donald Trump has released various social media comments throughout the day, preaching about winning the war. The posts come as the US and Iran both warn they are ready to escalate, casting doubt on ceasefire negotiations.

insights.headline: Even Trump's most basic claims about the Iran war can't be trusted
insights.summary: A CNN analysis debunks several of Trump's claims about the Iran war, including casualty figures, operational success claims, and statements about allied support. Raises serious questions about administration credibility.

positive: Ukraine has a plan to build back better with green reconstruction, signalling resilience despite war fatigue
Clean energy met 100% of world's new power needs in 2025, reducing vulnerability to oil supply shocks
Singapore dollar seen as safe haven amid Iran war uncertainty, demonstrating safe-haven demand for Asian currencies
negative: Foreign Policy: The Iran war is devastating for the global south, with energy price spikes hitting hardest in developing economies
ADB chief warns Japan's too-slow rate hikes are creating yen pressure and economic vulnerability
Traders bracing for renewed turmoil on Hormuz standoff, with oil headed back toward $90/barrel
Iran war costs $50 billion in lost oil production after just 50 days, with sulfur/fertilizer supply chains also disrupted

patterns: Iran war dominates coverage: 6+ major stories directly tied to US-Israel-Iran conflict, with ceasefire talks in Pakistan stalled and both sides warning of readiness to escalate
Escalation signals: US (VP Vance ready to fly to Pakistan) and Iran both signal war readiness despite ceasefire framework announcement
Cyber escalation: Pro-Iran hacktivist groups increasingly targeting Western critical infrastructure (LA Metro hack claimed)
Economic spillover mounting: $50B+ in oil losses, Hormuz standoff reviving market uncertainty, energy prices threatening to return to $90+ range
US credibility questioned: Multiple outlets fact-check Trump administration claims on Iran war progress and casualties
US-China nuclear dialogue: Senator urges bringing China into nuclear arms control talks, signalling concern about arms race dynamics
Clean energy as strategic alternative: Clean power meeting 100% of new global electricity demand in 2025 is reframed as energy security solution amid Iran crisis
China legal shield: New Chinese regulations counter 'unjustified' extraterritorial application of foreign laws, signalling shift to legal warfare against external pressure
Regional impact spreading: Iraq unable to maintain neutrality, Lebanon ceasefire holding funerals, Sudan entering fourth year of war
blind_spots: Limited reporting on actual Iranian military capabilities and damage assessments — most figures come from anonymous officials or official claims
Pakistan's internal political situation barely covered despite being the supposed venue for ceasefire talks
Humanitarian dimension of Iran war on ordinary Iranians — health system, food security, refugee flows — largely absent from mainstream coverage
Russian and Chinese roles in the conflict underreported — both have clear strategic interests but coverage focuses almost entirely on US/Iran dynamic
Israeli civilian impact and domestic political fallout from prolonged conflict rarely examined
Intellectual property and technology transfer implications of the war for AI/tech sector not explored
African and Latin American reactions to the global south energy crisis largely ignored
No coverage of public opinion polling in any of the directly involved nations
